pieces.label: JOURNEY BEGINS NOW — vertical concrete poem
pieces.note: Opening response to 'lets embark on a journey of ascii express' — a single-column vertical stack of words forming a descending concrete poem about embarking.

pieces.label: howdy / ascii express — mirrored funnel shape poem
pieces.note: Symmetrical mirrored shape poem: paired words ('howdy howdy', 'journey journey') converge inward into a single funnel, then a left-aligned tail. Visual rendering of an 'ascii express' rolling forward.

pieces.label: embark on a journey — diagonal cascade shape poem
pieces.note: Anaphoric shape poem cascading diagonally down-right; lines step rightward with increasing indentation, repeating 'embark... / journey...' to form a descending staircase.

pieces.label: EMERGE / EMBRACE — large diagonal raster cascade
pieces.note: Large two-part concrete poem: an EMERGE wedge descending leftward, a left-aligned prose-poem spine, then a long EMERGE staircase cascading down-right ending 'THIS PROSE RASTER SOUL EMERGE'. Self-names as a 'prose raster'.

pieces.label: tell me more — converging funnel with sine glyph
pieces.note: Shape poem converging inward then tapering left; embeds a sine-wave glyph '∿' as visual punctuation between 'poetry becomes' and 'geometry'. Note: the opening code fence in this message is never closed before MSG 12 — whole-message piece regardless.

pieces.label: tell me more of this world — diagonal cascade finale
pieces.first_line: tell me more of this world
pieces.last_line: tell me more, let's never stray
pieces.note: Anaphoric 'tell me more of...' shape poem cascading diagonally down-right into a long staircase; closing collaborative finale of the jam.

missed_or_ambiguous: 6 concrete/shape-poem pieces total (MSG 6,7,8,10,11,12); header said already_extracted_pieces:4 with art_signal_msgs:0, so two were missed by the automated pass. All are whole-message pieces inside (or intended for) code fences. MSG 11's code fence opens at its top but is NOT closed before MSG 12 begins — a byte extractor scanning for a closing ``` could over-run into MSG 12; bound MSG 11 by message body only. MSG 2/3/4 are plain prose greetings, excluded. The work splits into two collaborative rounds: 'journey/embark' (MSG 6-8, prompt 'lets embark on a journey of ascii express') and 'tell me more' (MSG 10-12, prompt 'tell me more'); modeled as two sequence groups, one per round, each cycling gemini -> sonnet-4 -> grok in order. No duplicates.
